label: ai logs for week 8
topic: Member 1 (Frontend Lead - Employee Experience) pages built from scratch, plus a temporary mock API for testing

content:
Attached 3 reference documents (HLD_LeaveManagementSystem_1.md, Implementation_Plan_LeaveManagementSystem.md, Leave_Management_System_UseCases_and_TaskAllocation_1.md) plus ~15 reference code files representing Member 4's existing client-side work (App.jsx, Login.jsx, Employee.jsx, Approver.jsx, http.js, dates.js, index.html, index.css, main.jsx, package.json, vite.config.js, tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js, .gitignore, README.md).

Request: "Based on these HLD, Implementation and Use Cases md files, create the pages required for member 1 whose role is [Frontend Lead — Employee Experience] and primary responsibilities is [Login, leave application form, employee dashboard, calendar view (FullCalendar.js or react-big-calendar), responsive layout (Tailwind). Integrates AI-1 natural-language input. Owns UI for UC-01, UC-03, UC-08, UC-09.]. Use the other files codes (everything except the first three MD files) as the start because these are from member 4's code, then add and remove any code so it becomes the pages for member 1's role. The README explains the structure. These other files are under the client folder for member 4's code."

Checked the working directory first — it was actually empty (only .claude/settings.local.json and an empty 'ai logs' folder); the attached HLD/Implementation Plan/Use Cases docs and reference code were conversation context only, never written to disk. Built the client/ app from scratch under the project's existing client/ + server/ naming convention (per its own README), scoped strictly to Member 1's ownership (UC-01, UC-03, UC-08, UC-09): Login, Dashboard, Leave Apply (AI-1 NL parsing + AI-2 coverage banner), Leave History, and a FullCalendar-based team Calendar. Split the monolithic reference Employee.jsx into pages/, components/{common,employee,calendar}/, services/, context/, hooks/ per the HLD's file layout. Added react-router-dom routing + ProtectedRoute, swapped ad hoc toast state for react-hot-toast, and deliberately excluded the Approver/Admin UI (Member 2's ownership) — non-employee roles see a placeholder instead. Verified with npm install && npm run build (clean, no errors).

actions.summary: Explored repo (find/ls) - confirmed the working directory was empty aside from .claude/ and 'ai logs/'
Created client/ scaffold: package.json, vite.config.js, tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js, index.html, .env.example, src/index.css, src/main.jsx
Created src/lib/dates.js and services/{api,authService,leaveService,calendarService,balanceService,aiService}.js
Created src/context/AuthContext.jsx and hooks/{useAuth,useLeave}.js
Created src/components/common/{LoadingSpinner,StatusBadge,ProtectedRoute,Navbar,Sidebar}.jsx
Created src/components/employee/{LeaveBalanceCard,LeaveApplicationForm,LeaveHistoryTable,OverlapWarningBanner}.jsx
Created src/components/calendar/{TeamCalendar,PublicHolidayLegend}.jsx using @fullcalendar/react
Created src/pages/{LoginPage,DashboardPage,LeaveApplyPage,LeaveHistoryPage,CalendarPage}.jsx
Created src/App.jsx: router shell + role gate + NonEmployeeNotice placeholder for non-employee roles
npm install && npm run build - clean build, 126 modules, no errors
Tracked the multi-step build via a todo list; all items completed

content: allow me to view the webite to check what has changed at each step

Started the Vite dev server (port 3000) and drove it with headless Chromium (Playwright) set up in a scratch directory since no browser-testing tool was preinstalled. Captured screenshots of the Login page (desktop + mobile) with no console errors, then - since there's no backend yet - mocked the relevant axios routes in-browser to render Dashboard, Apply (with AI-1 parse + AI-2 coverage warning), History, and Calendar with realistic fixture data, plus a mobile-viewport dashboard shot. All rendered correctly with zero console errors.

actions.summary: Read .claude/settings.local.json to check existing permissions
Started npm run dev in background, polled until it served on :3000
Invoked the 'run' skill for guidance on driving a browser-based app headlessly
Installed Playwright + Chromium (with OS deps) in a scratch npm project since chromium-cli wasn't available
Wrote drive.js and drive-full.js Playwright scripts to screenshot Login (desktop/mobile) and, via page.route() mocks, Dashboard/Apply/History/Calendar/mobile-dashboard
Ran the scripts, captured screenshots to a scratch screenshots/ folder, confirmed 'CONSOLE ERRORS: none' each time
Viewed each screenshot (login, dashboard, apply w/ AI-2 banner, AI-1 parsed JSON, history, calendar, mobile dashboard, full-page apply w/ coverage banner) to confirm correct rendering

content: add a mock api using axios for the time-being to test the website

Added a real in-browser mock API using axios-mock-adapter so the site is clickable end-to-end without waiting on the real backend. Built src/mocks/{data,coverage,parseNaturalLanguage,server}.js: 5 demo users (3 employees + supervisor + manager, password demo123!), seeded balances/holidays/requests, real working-day + overlap-conflict math for the AI-2 coverage check (with alternative-date suggestion), and an offline heuristic parser standing in for AI-1. Wired installMockApi(api) into services/api.js, on by default via VITE_USE_MOCK_API (defaults true without needing a .env file). Found and fixed a real bug while testing: the global 401 interceptor was force-redirecting on a failed login request too (also a 401), stomping the 'Invalid email or password' message before the user could see it - excluded /user/login from that redirect. Also discovered the mock's in-memory request list reset on every page refresh (no server process to hold state), so added localStorage persistence for it. Verified everything live in the browser: bad-password error stays visible, demo-account login round-trips through the mock, Apply's coverage-check flags a real conflict with a working 'Use suggested dates' button, submitted requests persist into History, cancel round-trips, Calendar renders team leave + holidays, non-employee roles hit the ownership-boundary placeholder, and a real page reload no longer wipes test data. Zero console errors across every route. Stopped the dev server and cleaned build artifacts afterward.

actions.summary: Checked latest axios-mock-adapter version on npm (2.1.0)
Created src/mocks/data.js - demo users, balances, holidays, seeded requests + localStorage persistence
Created src/mocks/coverage.js - working-day calc + AI-2 conflict/alternative-date logic
Created src/mocks/parseNaturalLanguage.js - AI-1 offline heuristic parser
Created src/mocks/server.js - axios-mock-adapter handlers for every endpoint the app calls
Edited services/api.js: installMockApi(api) call + fixed 401-redirect-on-login-failure bug
Edited package.json to add axios-mock-adapter; edited .env.example to document VITE_USE_MOCK_API
npm install, npm run build - clean
Restarted dev server; drove a full login to apply to history to cancel to calendar to logout to non-employee flow with Playwright against the live mock, screenshotting each step
Added persistMockRequests()/localStorage persistence to data.js and server.js after discovering refresh wiped mock state
Re-verified: a real in-tab page reload preserves the new request (fixed); final regression sweep across all 5 routes - zero console errors
Stopped the dev server, removed the dist/ build artifact

content:
Please write a handoff summary for this session. Include:
- What we were working on and the current state
- Key decisions made and the reasoning behind them
- Files created or modified
- What still needs to be done
- Any known issues or things to watch out for

Checked git status (nothing committed yet, branch T-X-TJor) and wrote a handoff summary covering: session context (repo was empty at start; docs were conversation-only, not on disk), current state (client/ builds clean, verified live), key decisions (client/ naming, HLD file-layout split, react-router-dom + FullCalendar, scope boundary excluding Member 2's Approval/Admin UI, client-side mock API with localStorage persistence, the 401-redirect bug fix), the full file tree created, what's left (real backend, turning the mock off later, Member 2's notification wiring, real AI-1 LLM call, no automated tests yet, deeper mobile audit), and known issues to watch for (static mock balances, fake mock JWTs, axios-mock-adapter shipping in the prod bundle unless disabled, 2 unaddressed npm audit findings, per-browser localStorage mock state, scratch Playwright scripts not persisting in the repo).

actions.summary: git status --short / git branch --show-current / file count check
